title: Anti-cryptosporidial activity of Camellia sinensis (green tea extract) in experimentally infected immunocompromised mice
author: El-Wakil, Eman S.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: Fig. 2. Agarose gel electrophoresis showing: Lane 1: 50 bp DNA molecular weight marker, Lane 2: Positive control, Lane 3: Negative control, Lane 4: Positive sample of nested PCR products targeting COWP gene of Cryptosporidium at 553 bp, Lane 5: 50 bp DNA molecular weight marker, and Lane 6: RFLP products of the sample after digestion with RsaI endonuclease (C. parvum genotype 2 digestion products at 410, 106, and 34 (too small to be detected) bp.
